info.name: Jira-Freshservice Bi-Directional Sync
info.description:
Production-grade collection for testing the full Jira <-> Freshservice bi-directional webhook sync.

Set ONE collection variable:
  base_url = http://localhost:3000/api

All credentials are loaded from your server's .env file — zero secrets stored here.

variable.description: Change to your ngrok / production URL for live testing.

request.description: Confirms the server is up. Returns 'Hello World!'.

Fires 'jira:issue_created'. Creates a Freshservice ticket.
Priority High → FS 3 | Status To Do → FS 2 (Open)
Status: In Progress → FS status 3 (Pending). Loop prevention active.
Summary and description synced to FS ticket subject/description.
Jira comment → Freshservice public note (HTML with author attribution).

⚙️ HOW IT WORKS:
1. Server downloads binary from Jira URL using JIRA_EMAIL + JIRA_API_TOKEN from .env
2. Re-uploads binary to Freshservice ticket as multipart/form-data note
3. Falls back to a link-note if download fails

📝 Replace 'content' URLs with real Jira attachment content URLs.

Creates a Jira issue.
FS priority 3 (High) → Jira 'High'
FS status 2 (Open) → 'To Do'

Status 4 (Resolved) → transitions Jira to 'Done'.
Dynamic transition lookup — no hardcoded IDs.
In your refactored setup, notes arrive via ticket_updated with a latest_note object. This test simulates that EXACT workflow.

⚙️ HOW IT WORKS:
1. Server downloads binary from FS URL using FRESHSERVICE_API_KEY from .env
2. Re-uploads binary to Jira issue via POST /rest/api/3/issue/:key/attachments
   with X-Atlassian-Token: no-check header
3. Falls back to ADF comment with download links if upload fails

📝 Replace 'attachment_url' values with real FS attachment URLs.
Handles the legacy freshdesk_webhook payload shape which often triggers a 'ticket_updated' classification with field changes.
